world.title: President Mahama heads to France for global health summit, Macron talks
world.description: President John Dramani Mahama is set to embark on an official visit to France, where he will participate in high-level global health discussions and hold bilateral talks with French leader Emmanuel Macron.

world.title: Iran issues chilling threat of 'devastation' as Trump sparks WW3 fears
world.description: Showing no signs of backing down Iran has warned of 'much more devastating' retaliation to Trump's earlier expletive-laden post.

world.title: Badenoch Ranked Most Popular UK Party Leader in New Poll Ahead of Upcoming Elections
world.description: British Conservative leader Kemi Badenoch has been ranked the most popular party leader in the United Kingdom, according to a new opinion poll, despite all major politicians recording negative approval ratings.

world.title: US and Iran receive peace proposal as Trump vows ‘hell’ if strait stays shut
world.description: The US president punctuated his threat with profanity in a social media post, saying that Tuesday will be “Power Plant Day, and Bridge Day, all wrapped up in one, in Iran”.

world.title: Danger at every step: F-15E airman's rescue mission is red flag for Trump's Iran plans
world.description: A wounded US F-15E gunner spent nearly two days hiding in Iran's rugged mountains as US special forces, drones and CIA deception tactics raced against time and an Iranian manhunt. While the rescue ended in a success, Washington allegedly blew up some of its own aircraft worth billions on Iranian soil. This episode is a costly preview of what Donald Trump's boots-on-the-ground plan for Kharg Island could look like.

world.title: Danish warship sunk by Nelson's fleet discovered after 225 years
world.description: The ship was sank in brutal hours-long naval clash, considered one of Nelson’s "great battles".

business.title: Nearly 50 Qatar LNG Tankers Sit Idle Across Asia
business.description: A wave of idled Qatari LNG carriers across Asia highlights a sharp disruption in global gas trade, driven by war, weakening demand, and shifting market dynamics.

business.title: BoG Governor highlights tough trade-offs in Central Bank decision-making

business.title: Traders, Freight forwarders push back against planned cargo tracking policy
business.description: The Coalition of Concerned Importers, Exporters, Traders and Freight Forwarders has urged the government to suspend all steps toward implementing the Electronic Cargo Tracking Note and Shipment Processing Note (ECTN/SPN) system in Ghana.

business.description: Governor of the Bank of Ghana (BoG), Dr Johnson Pandit Asiama, has underscored the difficult trade-offs involved in central bank decision-making, stressing the need to balance growth and inflation.

business.title: ICOTYDE Psoriasis Data And JNJ-4804 Funding Could Be A Game Changer For Johnson & Johnson (JNJ)
business.description:
In late March 2026, Johnson & Johnson reported 52-week Phase 3 data showing its oral peptide ICOTYDE maintained high complete skin clearance rates and a consistent safety profile in adults and adolescents with moderate-to-severe plaque psoriasis, while Royalty Pharma announced a US$500 million R&D co-funding agreement to advance JNJ-4804 for autoimmune diseases.
These developments highlight Johnson & Johnson’s push to build a differentiated immunology portfolio, pairing late-stage oral...

business.title: Jet fuel crisis: Rationing triggered at four airports in Italy
business.description: Four northern Italian airports introduce restrictions on jet fuel due to the energy crisis, with priority given to long-haul and medical flights. Meanwhile, Ryanair has warned that if the Iran war continues, summer flights are at risk across Europe.

business.title: Is the stock market correction a once-in-a-decade chance to target a million-pound SIPP?

science.title: Russia to develop tech to reach Mars
science.description: Russian Roscosmos chief Dmitry Bakanov said on Monday that his country is developing technologies that will enable it to send manned expeditions to Mars without endangering the astronauts, AzerNEWS reports.

science.title: How Bennu's Plumbing System Preserved the Solar System's Most Fragile Organics
science.description: Nanoscale analysis of a sample from asteroid Bennu has revealed evidence that water did not permeate the body uniformly during its formation but instead may have flowed through restricted channels, creating sharply defined chemical neighborhoods that preserved fragile organic compounds in some zones while transforming others into mineral-rich domains. Research findings suggest this rewrites assumptions […]

science.title: Artemis mission approaches lunar loop for first flyby since 1972
science.description: The Artemis astronauts entered the final phase of their run-up to a lunar loop on Monday, a tipping point of sorts that means the Moon's gravity is now having a stronger pull on the spacecraft than Earth's.

science.title: Study reveals bile as reservoir for microplastics in humans
science.description: Plastic pollution has become one of the defining environmental issues of modern life, and microplastics are now known to enter the body through food, drink, and air.

ai.title: Meta layoffs: around 200 employees to be terminated amid AI push
ai.description: Meta plans to cut around 200 jobs in Silicon Valley, adding to recent layoffs across teams like recruiting and Reality Labs. Earlier cuts exceeded 1,000 roles, with more reductions possible. Despite this, the company continues hiring and had about 79,000 employees at the end of 2025.

ai.title: 'Nothing stays hidden to our sight, though hidden by Google': Iran Threatens OpenAI’s $30B Stargate AI Hub in UAE
ai.description: Iran has threatened “complete and utter annihilation” of OpenAI’s $30B Stargate AI data center in Abu Dhabi. A video shows satellite imagery and claims hidden infrastructure can be tracked, signaling a new phase in modern conflict where data centers and cloud networks are frontline targets.
